description: city in Kumamoto prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kumamoto (熊本市, Kumamoto-shi; Japanese pronunciation: [kɯ.ma.mo.to, -toꜜ.ɕi]) is the capital city of Kumamoto Prefecture on the island of Kyushu, Japan.

description: revolt of disaffected samurai against the new imperial government, nine years into the Meiji Era
description_wiki: The Satsuma Rebellion, also known as the Seinan War (Japanese: 西南戦争, Hepburn: Seinan Sensō; lit. 'Southwestern War'), was a revolt of disaffected samurai against the new imperial government of the Empire of Japan, nine years into the Meiji era.

description: 1877 battle
description_wiki: The Battle of Tabaruzaka was a major battle of the Satsuma Rebellion.

description: chemical compound
samurai of the Satsuma domain, supreme commander of Japanese army, one of the three great nobles who led the Meiji Restoration (1828-1877)
description_wiki: Saigō Takamori (Japanese: 西郷 隆盛; 23 January 1828 – 24 September 1877) was a Japanese samurai and politician who was one of the most influential figures in Japanese history.

description: capital city of Hyogo Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kobe ( KOH-bay; Japanese: 神戸, romanized: Kōbe, pronounced [koꜜː.be] ), officially Kobe City (神戸市, Kōbe-shi; [koː.beꜜ.ɕi]), is the capital city of Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan.

description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: country in North America
description_wiki: Canada is a country in North America.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: capital city of the province of British Columbia, Canada
description_wiki: Victoria is the capital city of the Canadian province of British Columbia, located on the southern tip of Vancouver Island off Canada's Pacific coast.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

description: city in Okayama prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Okayama (岡山市, Okayama-shi; Japanese: [okaꜜjama]) is the capital city of Okayama Prefecture in the Chūgoku region of Japan.

description: city in southern California, United States
city in Santa Barbara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Guadalupe is a small city located in Santa Barbara County, California.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Kagoshima Prefecture (鹿児島県, Kagoshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ka.ɡo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ, ka.ŋo-]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Kyushu and the Ryukyu Islands.

description: overseas possessions of England and later the United Kingdom (1607–1997)
description_wiki: The British Empire comprised the dominions, colonies, protectorates, mandates, and other territories headed by the British monarchy.

description: family name
family name
description_wiki: Asano (written: 浅野, 淺野 or hiragana あさの) is a Japanese language surname.

description: Japanese family name (田中)
description_wiki: Tanaka (たなか) is the fourth most common Japanese surname.
